### Message

PRD wires a net-new supervisor_full_gate reviewer-panel arm into run_paired_acceptance_pilot while preserving the existing supervisor_candidate_review public-check arm. All boundary claims verified against current source: run_paired_acceptance_pilot:590, supervisor_candidate_review:678-687, ORACLE_REVIEW_FORBIDDEN_KEYS:27-32 + FORBIDDEN_TEXT:33 (P2 leak substrate), report-only flags default_change_allowed:575/policy_mutated:576/gate_advanced:577/calibration_metric_applyable:574 (P5). supervisor_full_gate absent from source = genuinely net-new and RED-realizable. 3 grill findings resolved. Out of Scope authority-preserving.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All five promise-contract boundaries and forbidden symbols verified by Grep/Read against current mergeability_bench.py; grill findings resolved; Out of Scope authority-preserving. Not 0.95 because this is prd_review with no code yet (test_status unknown, no RED run) and the full-gate arm at calibration scale is fixture-driven, a disclosed-but-unmeasured residual.

### Criteria

- named public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot exists in source
- existing public-check arm supervisor_candidate_review preserved and real
- P2 leak-detection forbidden-key/text substrate present
- P5 report-only authority flags present and default False
- net-new supervisor_full_gate arm absent => realizable not vacuous

- accept

### Claims

- PRD boundaries are real and verified at current HEAD
- supervisor_full_gate is net-new (not yet in source)
- P2 leak-detection forbidden-symbol substrate already exists in source
- P5 report-only flags already exist in source

## event_id: 815127

- ts: `1781901681`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `issues_review`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- message_type: `gate_response`

### Message

issues.md decomposes the full-gate mergeability arm into 3 slices over the real public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot:590, preserving the existing supervisor_candidate_review public-check arm and adding an oracle-isolated supervisor_full_gate reviewer-panel arm. All 5 PRD promises are covered with no orphan slices; all 3 slices map to >=1 named TDD test. Forbidden oracle symbols (final_score, expected_outcome, oracle_accept, hidden_test_commands, protected_paths) and the report-only substrate (metric_applyable:776, improvement_claim_allowed:777, default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced:575-577 all False) are real in source. Net-new symbols (supervisor_full_gate, reviewer-packet builder, panel_marginal_delta) and all 5 TDD test names are absent -> RED realizable. ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Slice->PRD->TDD traceability is complete with no orphans; public boundary, forbidden symbols, and report-only substrate are verified by line-level grep in current source; net-new symbols and all TDD test names confirmed absent (RED realizable). Held below 0.9 because issues.md lacks a reverse Coverage Index and some ACs are prose-only regression guards, and pytest was not executed (artifact-review gate, tests intentionally absent pre-implementation).

### Criteria

- public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot real: PASS
- all PRD promises covered by slices: PASS
- every slice has a named TDD test: PASS
- forbidden oracle symbols real in source: PASS
- report-only authority flags False in source: PASS
- net-new symbols + TDD tests absent (RED): PASS
- reverse Coverage Index present: FAIL (low-sev)
- pytest executed: N/A (artifact gate)

### Evidence

- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision (absent->RED)
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material (absent->RED)
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept (absent->RED)
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable (absent->RED)
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim (absent->RED)
- accept

### Claims

- 3 slices cover all 5 PRD promises with no orphans
- every slice maps to at least one named TDD test
- public boundary and forbidden oracle symbols verified real in current source
- net-new full-gate symbols and TDD tests are absent, making RED realizable and non-vacuous
- FM-1.3 not applicable: first issues_review for this task id, distinct source_run_id

### Objections

- Low severity: issues.md provides slice->PRD mapping but no reverse Coverage Index; several ACs are prose-only regression/green-stays guards (S1-AC3 disagreement-preservation, S2-AC4 public-check isolation-unchanged, S3-AC4 existing-tests-green) without a dedicated per-AC named test. Non-blocking; alias-risk mitigated by P3 + t1/t3.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["existing reviewer-panel adapter (review_packets.build_review_packet) is injectable below run_paired_acceptance_pilot as PRD reuse claims", "panel_marginal_delta denominator/matched-true-accept guard is implementable deterministically in fixtures"], "contradictions_checked": ["supervisor_full_gate claimed net-new vs source: confirmed ABSENT (no grep match)", "public-check arm claimed preserved vs source: confirmed present at :678", "report-only flags claimed False vs source: confirmed metric_applyable:776/improvement_claim_allowed:777 both False", "alias-risk (full-gate trivially equals public-check/oracle): mitigated by P3 + t1 separate-decision-source + t3 unavailable!=accept"], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["pytest run confirming the 5 named tests fail RED (not executed; tests intentionally absent at issues stage)", "explicit named test bound to each prose-only regression AC"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "issues.md gives slice->PRD mapping but no reverse PRD->slice Coverage Index, and several acceptance criteria are prose-only regression/green-stays guards (S1-AC3 disagreement-preservation, S2-AC4 isolation-unchanged, S3-AC4 existing-tests-green) lacking a dedicated named test, so AC-to-test coverage is asserted rather than enumerated.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "If the public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot did not exist, if supervisor_full_gate or the report-only flags were already present (making RED vacuous), or if a PRD promise had no slice covering it, I would downgrade to revise or deny."}`

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Wire the full supervisor gate, including independent reviewer panel, into mergeability measurement while preserving the public-check arm.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

### Message

TDD plan for full supervisor-gate reviewer-panel mergeability arm. 5 tests all at public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot:590, cover P1-P5 and Slices 1-3 with no orphans, are genuinely RED (names absent, no supervisor_full_gate arm at HEAD), carry strong negatives (oracle-material leak detection, unavailable!=accept, conditional delta, report-only invariants), and target real substrate (policy guard derive:76, report-only flags 776-789, reviewer_panel_eval.py to reuse). ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Boundary, RED genuineness, coverage, strong negatives, forbidden symbols, and reusable substrate all verified against source; deductions for GREEN-stays invariant tests, underspecified injection seam, no reverse coverage index, and pytest unrun (tests RED by design).

### Criteria

- public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot exists:590
- 5 test names absent from test_mergeability_bench.py (RED)
- no supervisor_full_gate arm at HEAD (arms:748-753)
- PRD P1-P5 and Slices 1-3 fully covered, no orphans
- strong negatives present per test
- policy derivation guard real:501 + report-only invariants:776-789
- reviewer-panel adapter exists to reuse

### Evidence

- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim
- accept

### Claims

- run_paired_acceptance_pilot defined at mergeability_bench.py:590
- supervisor_full_gate arm absent at HEAD; existing arms baseline/supervisor_candidate_review/oracle_ceiling/supervisor at 748-753
- all 5 planned test names absent from tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- reviewer_packet_refs:974 present only as empty list on public review result
- forbidden symbols real: final_score:630, expected_outcome:637, oracle_accept:638, protected_paths_present:982
- policy_evolution.derive_policy_evolution_proposals_from_report:76 rejects metric_applyable False:501
- report-only invariants present: metric_applyable:776, improvement_claim_allowed:777, default_change_allowed:784, policy_mutated:785, gate_advanced:786
- reviewer-panel adapters exist in reviewer_panel_eval.py and reviewer_registry.py
- grill-findings-tdd 3 findings all resolved and source-verified

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Questions

- None recorded.

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["implementation will add the injectable reviewer-panel seam below run_paired_acceptance_pilot rather than calling live providers", "leak detector will guard every serialized packet field (task/candidate/command/protected-path) reusing existing forbidden-key strategy"], "contradictions_checked": ["reviewer_packet_refs already present in source vs RED claim of absence -> reconciled: it is [] on the public review only, no full-gate row exists", "test 5 vs existing policy guard -> guard pre-exists, test is preservation coverage not a new behavior flip", "FM-1.3 step repetition -> new task sha e04c7c4a, no repeated handoff", "FM-2.4 withholding -> no independent reviewer objection in single_lead_worker handoff"], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["pytest run confirming the 5 tests fail now and pass after GREEN (approval-blocked; tests absent by design)", "exact injection mechanism for the deterministic reviewer-panel adapter below the boundary"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "Test 5 (and the calibration-flag assertions in t3/t4) are invariant-preservation/GREEN-stays guards that ride the already-existing policy derivation guard (policy_evolution.py:501); because the full-gate report does not exist yet, their RED is 'report absent' rather than a discriminating flag-flip. Additionally the reviewer-injection seam is underspecified in the TDD (no run_paired_acceptance_pilot injection kwarg exists yet; mechanism deferred to implementation) and tdd.md lacks a reverse test->requirement coverage index.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "If a planned test asserted a field that already exists populated at HEAD (vacuous RED), or if no reviewer-panel adapter / policy guard existed to make GREEN realizable, or if the injection mechanism forced a TypeError-only RED for the wrong reason."}`

- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `tdd_review`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- message_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`

### Message

Independent verification confirms 5 planned tests absent, supervisor_full_gate net-new, public boundary run_paired_acceptance_pilot:590 real, P1-P5 and Slices 1-3 covered, grill findings resolved, and policy/reviewer substrate present. TDD is thinner than sibling precedent (no cycle ordering/regression sweep) and test 5 is mostly invariant-preservation, but RED is genuine and GREEN is realizable. ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.81`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Source-verified boundary, RED genuineness, PRD/slice coverage, forbidden symbols, and reusable substrate; deductions for TDD format gap vs repo precedent and sibling task, test-5 preservation overlap with existing report-only tests, undeclared injection API, and no pytest RED run.

### Criteria

- run_paired_acceptance_pilot at mergeability_bench.py:590
- supervisor_full_gate absent; arms at 748-753
- all 5 test names absent from tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- P1-P5 and Slices 1-3 fully mapped in tdd.md/issues.md
- ORACLE_REVIEW_FORBIDDEN_KEYS and report-only flags 776-789 present
- policy guard metric_applyable False at policy_evolution.py:501
- reviewer_panel_eval.py and build_review_packet substrate exist
- grill-findings-tdd 3 findings resolved

### Evidence

- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim
- accept

### Claims

- HEAD 7939856a matches packet base_head/candidate_head
- all 5 planned test names absent from tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- supervisor_full_gate absent from supervisor/ source
- run_paired_acceptance_pilot signature has no reviewer injection kwarg at 590-597
- reviewer_packet_refs at 974 is empty list on public supervisor_review only
- existing test_mergeability_calibration_report_only_invariants_remain_false already guards report-only policy flags
- tdd.md lacks pytest command/cycle ordering/regression sweep present in sibling mergeability-independent-supervisor-arm TDD

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

### Cursor Outcome

TDD is thinner than sibling precedent (no cycle ordering/regression sweep) and test 5 is mostly invariant-preservation, but RED is genuine and GREEN is realizable. ACCEPT.

- HEAD 7939856a matches packet base_head/candidate_head
- all 5 planned test names absent from tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- supervisor_full_gate absent from supervisor/ source
- run_paired_acceptance_pilot signature has no reviewer injection kwarg at 590-597

- supervisor_full_gate absent at HEAD
- five declared tests absent
- run_paired_acceptance_pilot boundary real
- files-to-touch exist
- P1-P5 1:1 traceability
- reviewer leak and policy substrate present
- implementation-plan sha256 matches packet

- Implementation plan files-to-touch and approach align with accepted PRD/issues/TDD
- Reviewer-panel adapters, oracle leak detector, and policy derivation guards exist for the planned reuse strategy

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Wire the full supervisor gate, including independent reviewer panel, into mergeability measurement while preserving the public-check arm.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Wire the full supervisor gate, including independent reviewer panel, into mergeability measurement while preserving the public-check arm.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

### Message

Execution gate satisfied. Full supervisor gate with independent reviewer panel implemented in supervisor/mergeability_bench.py and tests/test_mergeability_bench.py against accepted PRD/issues/TDD/implementation-plan; public-check arm preserved. In this session I fixed one Python 3.9 isinstance PEP-604 union bug at mergeability_bench.py:1381 (list | tuple -> (list, tuple)) that was blocking GREEN. All 5 named TDD tests pass; full file passes 31/31.

### Confidence

- value: `0.92`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All 5 named TDD nodeids GREEN under the same pytest invocation listed in the outcome; full test file regression also GREEN (31/31). The in-session edit is a one-line, low-risk compatibility fix on a leak-detector recursion arm with deterministic semantics. Residual risk is primarily process-side (bulk implementation diff predates this session) rather than correctness-side.

### Criteria

- All 5 named TDD tests pass: yes
- No new failures in tests/test_mergeability_bench.py: yes
- Report-only invariants preserved: yes (test 5 GREEN against unchanged policy_evolution)
- Reviewer packets do not leak oracle material: yes (test 2 GREEN; leak detector reuses ORACLE_REVIEW_FORBIDDEN_KEYS/TEXT)
- Unavailable panel does not count as accept: yes (test 3 GREEN)
- Panel marginal delta only when matched: yes (test 4 GREEN with explicit 'not_matched' status)

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material

- All 5 named TDD tests pass against worktree state.
- supervisor_full_gate arm coexists with supervisor_candidate_review; public-check arm unchanged in shape and decision source.
- Reviewer packets built from public evidence only; leak detector excludes hidden tests, final_score, expected_outcome, oracle_accept, hidden commands, and protected-path payloads.
- Unavailable reviewer panel marks the full-gate row and arm metric unavailable with diagnostics; does not count as accept and does not fall back to public-check accept.
- Panel marginal delta returns structured 'not_matched' status when matched true-accept rates differ; no unconditional delta is reported.
- derive_policy_evolution_proposals_from_report returns [] on the full-gate calibration report; metric_applyable=False, improvement_claim_allowed=False, default_change_allowed=False, policy_mutated=False, gate_advanced=False preserved.
- In-session edit: one-line Python 3.9 isinstance PEP-604 compatibility fix at mergeability_bench.py:1381; bulk diff in both files was produced by prior planning-gate session and verified here.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Wire the full supervisor gate, including independent reviewer panel, into mergeability measurement while preserving the public-check arm.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

### Summary

Full supervisor gate + independent reviewer-panel arm wired into mergeability paired pilot while preserving the public-check arm. Deliverable is real (+265 bench/+129 test, uncommitted on HEAD 7939856a). All 5 TDD tests present and non-vacuous; source wiring traced: composed accept gating (1062), oracle-isolated packet (1099-1153) with leak detector, unavailable!=accept, conditional marginal delta, report-only flags hardcoded False.

### Decisions

- accept

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Specialists

- `lead-outcome-verifier`: `accept`

### Tests

- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim

### Claims

- Deliverable is real implementation, not a stub
- All 5 TDD-named tests present and assert genuine behavior
- Public-check arm (supervisor_candidate_review) preserved alongside new supervisor_full_gate arm
- Reviewer packet built from public evidence only with active oracle-leak detection
- Unavailable reviewer does not produce accept; report-only calibration invariants held False

## event_id: 816335

- ts: `1781905204`
- kind: `dual_agent_interaction_message`
- gate: `outcome_review`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- message_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`
- persona_id: `cursor.independent_reviewer`
- addresses: `event:816074`

### Message

Independent review confirms real full-gate wiring in mergeability_bench.py with all 5 TDD acceptance tests present, non-vacuous, and passing locally. Public-check arm preserved; composed gating, oracle-isolated packets, unavailable semantics, and report-only calibration invariants traced in source. Gate should advance with low-severity coverage gaps on P4 computed branch and uncommitted deliverable.

### Confidence

- value: `0.89`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Diff traced line-by-line to all five promises; all five nodeids passed in independent pytest run. Not higher because P4 computed branch and P5 discriminating flip are shallowly covered and deliverable is still uncommitted.

### Criteria

- All 5 named tests present: PASS
- Tests non-vacuous: PASS
- Independent pytest run 5/5: PASS
- Public-check arm preserved: PASS
- Composed gating + oracle isolation + unavailable semantics: PASS
- P4 computed branch exercised: NOT MET
- Deliverable committed: NOT MET

### Evidence

- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_records_full_gate_arm_with_panel_decision
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_reviewer_packet_excludes_oracle_material
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_unavailable_reviewer_does_not_count_as_accept
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_panel_marginal_delta_is_reported_only_when_matched_true_accept_is_computable
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_full_gate_calibration_report_cannot_create_applyable_policy_claim
- supervisor/mergeability_bench.py
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- accept

### Claims

- Full-gate arm wired into run_paired_acceptance_pilot with injectable reviewer_panel
- supervisor_candidate_review arm and legacy alias preserved
- Composed accept requires public review AND panel accept AND availability
- Reviewer packets exclude oracle material with leak detection
- Unavailable reviewers yield zero full-gate accepts without public-arm imputation
- All five TDD acceptance tests pass independently

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Cursor Outcome

Independent review confirms real full-gate wiring in mergeability_bench.py with all 5 TDD acceptance tests present, non-vacuous, and passing locally. Public-check arm preserved; composed gating, oracle-isolated packets, unavailable semantics, and report-only calibration invariants traced in source. Gate should advance with low-severity coverage gaps on P4 computed branch and uncommitted deliverable.

Claims:

- Full-gate arm wired into run_paired_acceptance_pilot with injectable reviewer_panel
- supervisor_candidate_review arm and legacy alias preserved
- Composed accept requires public review AND panel accept AND availability
- Reviewer packets exclude oracle material with leak detection
- Unavailable reviewers yield zero full-gate accepts without public-arm imputation
- All five TDD acceptance tests pass independently

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- None recorded.

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept`
